featured.title: How to Order at a Berlin Bakery: The German Words and Habits That Get You Served
featured.summary: A German bakery order is short and follows the same pattern every time. Here is the one sentence that starts it, the Berlin words phrasebooks miss, the questions they ask back, and what to do when your German runs out.
featured.alt: A glass bakery counter in Berlin filled with croissants and filled rolls
featured.label: New: practical German

miniPosts.title: Baltic Sea Day Trip from Berlin: Which Beach to Pick and How the Trains Work
miniPosts.summary: The Baltic coast starts less than three hours north of Berlin by train. Here is how Warnemünde, the Usedom imperial resorts and Binz on Rügen compare for one sea day, which trains run on the Deutschlandticket, and the returns you must not miss.
miniPosts.alt: Rows of beach chairs on Warnemünde beach with the 1898 lighthouse and the curved Teepott roof behind

miniPosts.title: Berlin Pride 2026 (CSD): The Parade, the Whole Week, and the History Behind It
miniPosts.summary: Berlin Pride 2026 (CSD) holds its main parade on July 25. A local guide to the route, the whole Pride week, where to watch, and the history that makes Berlin a queer capital.
miniPosts.alt: Feathered dancers and a rainbow-flag truck at a crowded pride parade near the Brandenburg Gate under a bright blue sky

miniPosts.title: Köpenick Berlin: The Palace, the Lake and the Fake Captain Who Fooled Prussia
miniPosts.summary: Berlin’s oldest corner is an island town in the far southeast: a Baroque palace, a fishermen’s lane, the city’s biggest lake and the town hall a fake captain robbed in 1906. Here is how to plan the half day by S-Bahn, on one AB ticket.
miniPosts.alt: View across the Frauentog bay to the Köpenick old town island with its church and town hall towers

miniPosts.title: Berlin Public Transport for Tourists: Tickets, Zones, Prices and Validation
miniPosts.summary: Berlin public transport uses one shared ticket system for U-Bahn, S-Bahn, tram and bus. This local guide covers zones, prices, validation, BER airport trips, and the EUR 60 fine.
miniPosts.alt: Yellow train speeding through Konstanzer Strasse station. The wall has bold stripes and text. Ceiling lights illuminate the platform.

posts.title: Deutsches Technikmuseum Berlin: The Giant Museum With a Plane on the Roof
posts.summary: A full-size C-47 sits on a roof in Kreuzberg, and under it waits Berlin’s biggest museum complex: 40+ aircraft, two real 1874 roundhouses, a giant ship hall and a hands-on science centre on one ticket. Here is how to plan it without losing a day.
posts.alt: The Douglas C-47 Rosinenbomber mounted on the roof of the Deutsches Technikmuseum's glass New Building

posts.title: Public Toilets in Berlin: Where to Find Them, What They Cost, and Free Options
posts.summary: Need a toilet in Berlin? This tourist guide shows where to find public toilets, what they cost, where the free options are, and when stations, malls, or the Berliner Toilette app help.
posts.alt: Restroom sign with male and female symbols on a brick wall, pointing right. Green trees and glass buildings in the background.

posts.title: Is Berlin Walkable? How Far Apart the Main Sights Really Are
posts.summary: Is Berlin walkable? The central sights are far closer than the map suggests, and flat underfoot. Here is how far apart they really are, the one walk to do on foot, and when to take the train instead.
posts.alt: The Bode Museum dome and the TV Tower rising across the Spree on Berlin's Museum Island, two of the central sights that sit within an easy walk of each other
